pattern: Build a per-team position-vote vector, then sort teams by that vector descending, breaking ties alphabetically — a custom comparator over the vote counts handles the full ranking in one pass.
hints: For each team, count how many voters placed it first, second, third, and so on — that gives a ranking vector per team.
Teams should be sorted so that a team with more first-place votes comes first; on a tie, compare second-place votes, and so on down the positions.

If two teams are still tied after comparing all positions, rank them alphabetically. Build the count table then sort teams using the negated vote vector as the key, with the team letter as a final tiebreaker.

edge_inputs.description: split votes produce tie resolved by position counts
two teams, tie at position 1 resolved by position 2
single voter defines the entire ranking
all teams tied on every position — sort alphabetically
three teams with complex vote splitting

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    teams = list(string.ascii_uppercase[:10])
    votes = []
    for _ in range(size):
        perm = teams[:]
        rng.shuffle(perm)
        votes.append(''.join(perm))
    return [votes]
